  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    This is how much coaching counts: Morocco should have no business in the semifinals, but if you give them the first 45 minutes free (66% possession by the Portuguese back line instead of getting the ball to your best player, Goncalo Ramos; an attacking section without any discipline - Joao Felix screwing up a 100% chance that Ramos arriving right behind him would put away), if your attacking improvement in the 2nd half comes down to adding -2 individuals (C. Ronaldo and Cancelo, so now you are playing 9 against 11) and then if in the final 20 minutes all your attacking prowess comes down to centering high balls into Dias (a defender, which is a sign of total cluelessness and lack of confidence!), then a weak team such as Morocco will take the chance. What else can you say about a team (Morocco) that couldn't even score on two 100% counterattacks, to put to bed the game with at least one more goal??? It is sad that teams such as Croatia, Argentina, and Morocco are the best at the momen

  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    Portugal (Fernando Santos really) does everything to give away this game to Morocco: 1. Why on Earth is Diogo Costa is the first choice goalkeeper when both Rui Patricio and Sa' are much better than him (the goal was Costa's fault)? 2. Why is Joao Felix playing sometimes on the left wing, sometimes as an attacking midfielder through the middle, and sometimes as a forward OVERLAPPING WITH GONCALO RAMOS??? (yes, you guessed it right: in the first few minutes he screwed up an enormous chance when he headed the ball into Bono, when Goncalo Ramos was right behind him and would have surely scored! If the game starts with an early Goncalo Ramos goal just like against Switzerland, the entire game would have been different! But NO, Joao Felix has total freedom on the pitch, I JUST DON'T KNOW WHY???) 3. Why is the clueless Portuguese defence is responsible for most of the 66% possession of Portugal??? Haven't they learned ANYTHING from the Spanish fiasco against Morocco???

  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    Parking the bus against Portugal won't be such a great idea as it was against the Spaniards. Portugal is loaded with dangerous forwards both in the lineup and on the bench, so parking the bus would be inviting disaster! The Spanish team was an Old Boys style team with no threat going forward (with Morata on the bench), so parking the bus against them worked. Portugal has threats of all kinds: headers (Pepe), inside the box (Goncalo Ramos), penalty (B. Fernandes), long distance shots (B. Silva, Guerrero, Felix, Cancelo, R. Horta, R. Leao). Not to mention that the greatest opportunity waster of all, Cristiano Ronaldo, is on the bench finally! When he was on, he wanted to party alone. Without him, every player wants to be part of the party, which translates into goals!

    3 years ago
     +2 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us

  • [ - ] Icarax - member since 27-Sep-17

    morocco are not dominant in midfield or attack they rely on counters and individual runs to some extent though they can have some nice combinations, they had more clear cut chances than spain did in the ro16. their strength is definitely the defense, every player there makes good decisions, is decisive and aggressive with precise tackling, and as such theyve conceded no goals so far this wc except an own goal i believe. portugal on the other hand are more all-rounded and showcased their sharpness and quality in all areas in the ro16, they wont be able to rip apart the moroccan team which actually has some structure unlike switzerland, i expect mostly a closed game like the one vs spain, morocco will create a couple chances but not necessarily score, same for portugal but their superior quality + more likely domination may get them there in the end, 0-0/0-1
